commandsRun: rm -rf .turbo && DATABASE_URL=... AWS_ENDPOINT_URL=... pnpm test (x2 cold)
implReport.notes: Invariants proven real PG: (a) emails_sent rollup DERIVES from messages_log SET-to-truth (ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE SET value=EXCLUDED.value), idempotent, heals drifted counter (999->7), workspace-scoped; (b) cost view sums penny-exact to direct_total+fixed_total, same active set (status='active') as denominator AND iterated rows, $24.95 IP only on upgraded; (c) advisor all-criteria-AND recommends only sustained-100k+cadence+healthy, rejects spikes, persists ip_recommendation WITHOUT changing ip_mode; (d) upgrade shared->warming->dedicated via jsonb merge preserving DKIM/config_set, SES-provision-first (failure leaves shared). Added provisionDedicatedIp(poolName) to SesEmailClient (CreateDedicatedIpPool) -> updated 5 concrete fakes for typecheck. billingUsage returns computed cost view + ip_recommendation/ip_mode badge, preserves existing usage array (web billing + role-visibility e2e unaffected, marketer still 403). Fixed pool $40 (METERING_FIXED_TOTAL env). No migration (usage_counters exists; ip fields in sending_identity jsonb). Did NOT run web Playwright (billing UI unmodified, only additive API; marketer-403 covered by router-role-enforcement).
implReport.testSummary: Cold full pnpm test TWICE: both green, 40/40 turbo tasks, no regression phases 2-12. Metering 51 tests (9 files) incl real-PG integration (rollup-reconcile, cost-view-sums-to-total, ip-advisor-inputs, ip-mode-warmup-transitions). @cdp/email ses-provision unit. local-api 27 incl new billing-cost-view + unchanged router-role-enforcement. web 33.

invariants: Tenant isolation: every tenant-scoped row carries workspace_id NOT NULL; every query filters by workspace_id. Service-role Lambdas bypass RLS so they MUST scope by workspace_id in code; the admin app uses the user JWT so RLS applies.

workspace_id is NEVER from a client payload — it comes from the API-key/authorizer context or a trusted server-set field. The frontend sends the active-workspace claim via its JWT; the API resolves scope from the authorizer, never from a body field.

Role enforcement (§3A): owner/marketer/accounting are workspace-scoped; system-admin is the only cross-tenant role and every cross-tenant access is audit-logged. The UI shows only what the role permits; the API enforces it regardless of the UI.

All sends (broadcasts §9A and automation actions §9B) go through the SAME outbox -> Dispatcher pipeline (§9) and pass suppression -> frequency-cap -> quiet-hours; sending is gated on workspace active/verified.

Idempotency: broadcasts dedupe per (broadcast_id, profile_id); the automation runner must tolerate retries without double-advancing (optimistic checks / updated_at); image uploads + metering rollups are idempotent.

LOCAL-only: AWS (SES/SNS/S3/SQS) are MOCKED (aws-sdk-client-mock) or run on LocalStack; never hit real AWS. Do NOT mock Postgres in the integration tier — use the real local DB on localhost:5433. The editor must emit MJML, never hand-rolled email HTML.

Logic in pure injected functions; Lambda handlers stay thin. Reuse the prior harness (packages/db testutil: adminPool, hasDatabaseUrl, applyMigrations; runPlanInWorkspaceTx; SqlStatement {text,values}; @cdp/segments resolveAudience; @cdp/email; the dispatcher core).

Determinism: turbo test task is cache:false — keep it. Unique workspace UUIDs + file-local namespaces per integration file (events.event_id / ses_message_id are GLOBAL keys). No cross-file races. Frontend e2e (Playwright) must be deterministic (seeded data, stable selectors).
Scope discipline: implement exactly what the cited sections require for THIS phase — no features beyond the spec. Keep the cost model EXACTLY as §20 (direct usage + even split of fixed costs).
context.loop: Backend phase: pure unit tests + DB-integration against REAL local Postgres (localhost:5433); mock AWS (aws-sdk-client-mock) / LocalStack. No frontend.

criteria: Per-workspace emails_sent / events_ingested reconcile with messages_log / events.
The cost view = direct usage cost + an equal share of fixed costs; per-workspace figures sum to the true total (worked example from §20 holds).
The IP-advisor recommends a dedicated IP only when sustained-volume + cadence + reputation criteria are all met; one-off spikes do not trigger it.
The upgrade-ip flow provisions a dedicated IP, warms it gradually (split routing), and tracks ip_mode/warmup_status; the $24.95 lands only on upgraded workspaces.
scope: services/metering: a scheduled rollup job populating usage_counters (emails_sent from messages_log, events_ingested, image bytes) and a per-workspace monthly cost computation using the EXACT §20 hybrid policy: direct/variable costs attributed to the workspace (emails_sent x $0.0001, dedicated IP $24.95 if upgraded, image bytes) PLUS an EQUAL share of the fixed pool (fixed_total / active_workspace_count). Per-workspace figures must sum to the true total.

A monthly IP-advisor job that recommends a dedicated IP only when ALL hold: sustained volume (~100k/mo for 2-3 consecutive months), consistent cadence, healthy reputation. Surfaces a recommendation (no auto-upgrade).
An upgrade-ip flow: provision a dedicated IP/pool, gradual warmup (split routing), track ip_mode/warmup_status per workspace; ~$24.95/mo becomes a direct cost only for upgraded workspaces.
phase.specSections: §20 (metering into usage_counters, hybrid cost model: direct usage + even split of fixed costs; per-workspace cost view), §10 (dedicated-IP recommendation engine + upgrade-ip migration with warmup), §6 (usage_counters, messages_log, email_events)
phase.title: Usage metering + cost attribution + IP advisor

implUnits: 1. services/metering/src/usage.ts period helpers + reconcile-from-truth rollup builders
2. cost.ts computeDirectCost/evenShare/computeAllWorkspaceCosts (highest-value, sum-to-total)
3. advisor.ts decideIpRecommendation + input reads + buildIpRecommendationUpdate
4. ip-upgrade.ts planUpgradeIp/warmupSplit/chooseSendPool state machine
5. packages/email SesEmailClient add provisionDedicatedIp + prod impl
6. metering deps.ts (reader + runStatementsInWorkspaceTx + listActiveWorkspaceIds + ProdSes, mirror batch-eval)
7. core.ts rollup-all + advisor-all orchestrators (per-workspace tx)
8. handler.ts + index.ts thin EventBridge-scheduled
9. dispatcher: route send via chooseSendPool/warmupSplit during warmup (split routing) + pool selection in buildSendEmailInput
10. local-api: GET /billing/usage returns computed cost view (sums to total) + ip_recommendation badge (read-only, accounting/owner)
plan.migration: NO new migration required. usage_counters exists, metric is free-text. ip_mode/warmup_status/dedicated_ip/ip_pool/ip_recommendation live in workspaces.sending_identity jsonb (§10A 'no new table'). Optional 0008 only to backfill sending_identity ip_mode:'shared' defaults (hardening, not required).
mocks: SES dedicated-IP provisioning (CreateDedicatedIpPool/PutDedicatedIpInPool/config-set IpPoolName) via aws-sdk-client-mock; extend SesEmailClient provisionDedicatedIp; never real
plan.notes: CRITICAL reconciliation gotcha: emails_sent ALREADY incremented per-send by dispatcher (value+1) and image bytes additive by image Lambda. Rollup MUST DERIVE authoritative monthly total from messages_log/events and write SET-to-truth (ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE SET value=EXCLUDED.value), NOT add, else double-count and AC1 fails. image_egress_bytes has no current writer (CloudFront-log derived) -> treat as 0. Cost sum-to-total is the segment-compiler-equivalent highest-value unit target. active_workspace_count = status='active' (excludes onboarding/suspended) and MUST be the same set for both even-split denominator and iterated rows or sum-to-total breaks. Advisor only RECOMMENDS (persists ip_recommendation); upgrade owner-triggered separately, never automatic (§10). Reuse SqlStatement + batch-eval deps pattern (scheduled, per-workspace tx, service-role bypass -> bind workspace_id $1). SES only mocked boundary.

assertions: buildEmailsSentRollup binds workspace_id $1 + month, COUNT messages_log status=sent in [period,period+1mo), SET-to-truth upsert ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE SET value=EXCLUDED.value (NOT +EXCLUDED, derives authoritative, no double-count vs dispatcher)
buildEventsIngestedRollup same on events.received_at
throws falsy workspaceId
monthBucket UTC YYYY-MM-01
leaves image bytes rows untouched
computeDirectCost emails*0.0001 + (24.95 if upgraded) + imageBytesCost
evenShare(40,5)=8, guards count=0
§20 example: small 10k shared ~9, large 300k upgraded ~63
SUM-TO-TOTAL: sum_i cost === sum_i direct + fixedTotal exactly (penny allocation, 40.01/3 no drift)
$24.95 only on ipUpgraded
image priced per $/GB, zero->0
recommend ONLY when ALL: sustained ~100k/mo 2-3 consecutive months + consistent cadence + healthy reputation
all met -> recommend:true + rationale
volume met but single-blast cadence -> false
volume+cadence but high bounce -> false
one-off spike (single 300k among 5k) -> false
1 sustained month false, 2 consecutive true
pure, never auto-upgrades
thresholds injected
planUpgradeIp ip_mode shared->dedicated/warming + warmup_status, merges sending_identity || $2::jsonb no clobber, workspace_id $1 one row
warmupSplit monotonic ramp over 2-4 weeks 0<share<=1 -> complete at 1.0
chooseSendPool deterministic by profile_id hash (retries route same)
buildIpRecommendationUpdate persists ip_recommendation without flipping ip_mode
no dedicated transition without explicit upgrade
provisionDedicatedIp calls SES dedicated-IP-pool boundary (CreateDedicatedIpPool/PutDedicatedIpInPool/config-set IpPoolName) via aws-sdk-client-mock, never real
DB dedicated transition only AFTER SES provisioning resolves; failure leaves ip_mode=shared (no half-upgrade)
SES only mocked boundary
real PG: seed messages_log(sent)+events 2 months 2 workspaces, rollup -> usage_counters.emails_sent==COUNT(messages_log), events_ingested==COUNT(events) per (workspace,period)
re-run idempotent (SET-to-truth, not doubled despite dispatcher increments)
tenant isolation: A counters only A's rows
month bucketing boundary correct
real PG: 5 active workspaces (one dedicated, small 10k, large 300k), compute cost view
each = direct + fixedTotal/5; small~9 large(upgraded)~63 within cent
SUM all costs === total direct + fixedTotal exactly (load-bearing)
$24.95 only on upgraded workspace breakdown
active_workspace_count excludes suspended/onboarding from denominator AND rows
real PG: build series from usage_counters (volume) + email_events (reputation) + messages_log distinct send-days (cadence), workspace-scoped
2-3 consecutive ~100k + daily + low bounce -> ip_recommendation persisted recommend:true
one-off spike -> none
high-bounce high-volume -> none; per-workspace isolation
real PG: upgrade-ip (SES mocked) -> sending_identity ip_mode shared->dedicated + warmup_status on one row
advancing warmup increases dedicatedShare -> complete cutover
$24.95 now in cost view for this workspace, NOT sibling shared
isolation: B sending_identity untouched

instructions: Clear the turbo cache and RUN the tests yourself (DATABASE_URL set) twice from cold, plus pnpm typecheck and pnpm lint. For UI phases, RUN the Playwright e2e in a real browser. Record real pass/fail. Confirm the integration tier RUNS (not skipped) and no regression in earlier phases.

Verify EACH acceptance criterion is covered by a passing, non-vacuous test: ["Per-workspace emails_sent / events_ingested reconcile with messages_log / events.","The cost view = direct usage cost + an equal share of fixed costs; per-workspace figures sum to the true total (worked example from §20 holds).","The IP-advisor recommends a dedicated IP only when sustained-volume + cadence + reputation criteria are all met; one-off spikes do not trigger it.","The upgrade-ip flow provisions a dedicated IP, warms it gradually (split routing), and tracks ip_mode/warmup_status; the $24.95 lands only on upgraded workspaces."].

Highest-priority checks: (a) tenant isolation + role enforcement (server-side, not just UI-hidden); (b) all sends route through the Dispatcher guards; (c) idempotency (broadcast dedupe / no double-advance / idempotent rollups); (d) AWS mocked/LocalStack (no real AWS) yet asserted; (e) for UI, the flow actually works in a real browser against the real backend, not a stub.
If any integration test mocks Postgres, that is a FAIL. If a UI criterion is only unit-tested (no real browser), that is a FAIL.
Scope check: flag any feature beyond the cited sections; verify the §20 cost model is exact.
Score 0-100. Set testsPass and criteriaAllMet honestly. Give concrete prioritized recommendations.
prompt.outputFormat: JSON: { score: number, testsPass: boolean, criteriaAllMet: boolean, criteriaCoverage: [{criterion, met, evidence}], isolationOk: boolean, browserVerified: boolean, scopeCreep: string[], recommendations: string[], criticalIssues: string[] }
prompt.role: adversarial principal reviewer + QA engineer verifying spec-fidelity, tenant isolation, roles, and (for UI) real-browser behavior
prompt.task: Independently verify "Usage metering + cost attribution + IP advisor" against its §18 criteria. Re-run the tests yourself (cold cache); do not trust the self-report.
